title: Campanopsis prostrata
intro: Campanopsis prostrata, commonly known as the creeping bellflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Campanopsis prostrata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. Campanopsis prostrata is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, and infusion in traditional herbal medicine, although its use is not widely recognized or standardized in modern Western medicine.
taxonomy: Campanopsis prostrata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. It is native to Japan and is also known as the Japanese campanopsis. The classification of Campanopsis prostrata is further broken down to the order Lamiales, subclass Asteridae, and class Magnoliopsida. This classification places Campanopsis prostrata within the larger group of flowering plants. As a member of the Oleaceae family, Campanopsis prostrata is closely related to other plants like forsythias and olives.
